summary: Decompiled and matched a substantial slice of Samus charge shot behavior plus two Samus bomb animation callbacks, while also standardizing repeated full-turn angle constants through a new M_TAU macro. The bot report credited 9 new 100% matches totaling +2776 bytes, including itsamuschargeshot creation, destruction, pickup, reflected, shield-bounced, accessory/animation helpers, and itsamusbomb_UnkMotion1_Anim/2_Anim. Review centered on replacing hand-expanded inline code with existing inlines such as ABS, HSD_JObjSetRotationY, HSD_JObjSetScale, and itGetJObjGrandchild, consolidating duplicated bomb motion logic, and improving item/parent gobj naming.
what_changed: Added M_TAU to src/MSL/math.h and replaced many 2 * M_PI or M_PI * 2 expressions across camera, effects, fighters, items, lbvector, and particle code.

Filled in Samus charge shot functions in src/melee/it/items/itsamuschargeshot.c, including it_802B55C8, it_802B56E4, it_2725_Logic108_Destroyed, it_2725_Logic108_PickedUp, it_802B5CBC, itSamuschargeshot_UnkMotion8_Anim, it_802B5EDC, it_2725_Logic108_Reflected, and it_2725_Logic108_ShieldBounced.

Expanded itSamusChargeshot_ItemVars in itCharItems.h from sparse placeholders into offset-labeled fields through xE34 and added itSamusChargeShot_Attributes with lifetime and several float tuning fields.
Updated itsamuschargeshot.h prototypes to use Fighter_Part for it_802B55C8 and concrete void Item_GObj signatures for it_802B5CBC and it_802B5EDC.
Matched Samus bomb animation callbacks itSamusbomb_UnkMotion1_Anim and itSamusbomb_UnkMotion2_Anim by factoring common preprocessing and facing/rotation handling into inline helpers.
Renamed spawn helper locals/parameters in Samus bomb code from ambiguous gobj/n usage to parent_gobj and Item_GObj* gobj.
Changed ftLib_80086630 return type from void* to HSD_JObj* in both implementation and header, with a cast retained at an existing byte-offset use site in itlinkarrow.c.
Charge shot reflected and shield-bounced logic now normalizes angles with M_TAU, updates facing direction, mirrors velocity on shield bounce, and uses HSD_JObjSetRotationY.
smart_moves: Factored repeated Samus bomb lifetime/rotation code into itSamusBomb_UnkMotion_PreProcess and itSamusBomb_UnkMotion_Process instead of duplicating two 512-byte animation functions.
Used existing HSD JObj inline wrappers such as HSD_JObjSetRotationY and HSD_JObjSetScale rather than keeping expanded assert/dirty-matrix sequences.
Introduced M_TAU once and reused it broadly, improving readability for angle normalization while preserving matching-sensitive float/double contexts.
Typed ftLib_80086630 as HSD_JObj* to reflect what the function actually returns, which simplified charge shot matrix/rotation access.
Used itGetJObjGrandchild for charge shot effect attachment/scaling instead of manually walking child->child with null checks.
Kept charge shot owner/parent relationships explicit through xE00 and owner checks before accessing fighter-owned state or resetting Samus/Kirby charge-shot state on destruction.
decomp_lessons: A local redeclaration of the same macro-derived pointer, such as a second GET_ITEM(gobj), can be evidence of an inline call boundary; try isolating with a scope block or creating an inline helper.

Hand-expanded jobj.h assert and dirty-matrix code often corresponds to an HSD_JObj inline. Reviewers suggested converting assert line numbers from hex to decimal and searching jobj.h for the invoked inline.
Prefer ABS(expr) for small absolute-value checks when the decompiled source shows manual negative-value handling around a threshold.
When two unmatched callbacks contain nearly identical code, test whether one calls the other or whether both call a shared inline helper; consolidation can improve matches and readability.
For item variable structs, local struct offsets in member names are preferred over generic x0/x4 names when semantic names are not known.
Use existing JObj helpers like HSD_JObjSetRotationY, HSD_JObjSetScale, and itGetJObjGrandchild to collapse large generated-looking code blocks.
For spawn helpers, distinguish the newly spawned item gobj from the parent/owner gobj in names because parent assignment fields reveal the relationship.
naming_conventions: Do not use vague locals like n for newly instantiated item objects; reviewers preferred gobj for the spawned item.
When a parameter is assigned into SpawnItem parent fields, name it parent_gobj rather than plain gobj if the function also creates/returns a child item gobj.
For unknown item-var fields, reviewer feedback preferred local offset names such as xDD8, xDDC, xE00 over struct-local x4/x8 when the fields map into item variable storage.
Concrete return types are preferred over void* when evidence is clear, as in ftLib_80086630 returning HSD_JObj*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use ABS inline checks for absolute velocity thresholds, e.g. !(ABS(ip->x40_vel.x) < 0.0001f).
Use scope blocks and shadowed locals to probe suspected inline boundaries before extracting an inline helper.
Replace expanded jobj.h code with HSD_JObjSetRotationY(jobj, 0.0f), HSD_JObjSetRotationY(jobj, M_PI), or HSD_JObjSetScale(grandchild, &scale) when assert-line evidence points to those inlines.
Convert jobj.h assert line constants such as 0x294U to decimal line numbers to identify the original inline in the header.
Normalize repeated full-turn angle loops with M_TAU once the constant is introduced, while preserving casts where the original code needs float or double behavior.
Consolidate duplicated animation callback bodies into shared inline helpers to recover matches without preserving duplicated decompiler output.
review_feedback: ribbanya identified a manual absolute-value sequence in itsamusbomb.c as an inline ABS check and suggested if (!(ABS(ip2->x40_vel.x) < 0.0001f)).
ribbanya noted that redeclarations such as another GET_ITEM(gobj) often indicate an inline function call and recommended scope blocks/name shadowing as a starting tactic.
ribbanya explained that expanded assert/dirty-matrix code from jobj.h should be replaced with the matching JObj inline; for line 0x294U/660 the suggestion was HSD_JObjSetRotationY(jobj, 0.0f).
ribbanya suggested HSD_JObjSetRotationY(jobj, M_PI) for the opposite-facing Samus bomb rotation case.
ribbanya suggested returning false instead of return 0 from bool callbacks.
ribbanya observed that Samus bomb UnkMotion1 and UnkMotion2 likely duplicated a direct call or shared inline and advised consolidating the duplicated code.
ribbanya objected to naming a spawned charge-shot object n and advised using gobj for the instantiated item and parent_gobj for the owner/parent argument.
ribbanya suggested replacing a manual child->child traversal with itGetJObjGrandchild.
ribbanya suggested replacing expanded JObj scale assignment and dirty-matrix code with HSD_JObjSetScale(grandchild, &scale).
jellejurre replied that the fixes were applied and specifically noted the jobj guidance made the code cleaner and easier.
ribbanya left a final struct naming note: using local struct offsets for member names is preferred.
follow_up_queries: Find other item files with manual child->child JObj traversal that can use itGetJObjGrandchild.
Search for expanded jobj.h assert/dirty-matrix patterns still needing HSD_JObjSetRotationY or HSD_JObjSetScale.
Search for remaining 2 * M_PI expressions that should use M_TAU if they preserve matching.
Audit itSamusChargeshot_ItemVars fields xDD8 through xE34 for future semantic names once behavior is better understood.
Check other fighter/item spawn helpers for ambiguous n locals or parent gobj naming issues.
